sections.heading: Surveillance
sections.content:
**Clinical Follow-Up Schedule:**
- Physical exam including complete skin and complete LN exam: Every 3-6 months for 3 years; every 6-12 months thereafter [MCC-6]
- Imaging and other studies as clinically indicated [MCC-6]
- Immunocompromised patients: more frequent follow-up may be indicated [MCC-6, footnote e]
- Self-examination of the skin recommended for patients with MCC due to increased risk for NMSCs [MCC-6]

**Imaging Strategy:**
- Surveillance imaging typically via diagnostic CT of chest/abdomen/pelvis with oral and IV contrast [MCC-6, footnote v]
- Neck CT often included if primary lesion was on head/neck [MCC-6, footnote v]
- Whole-body FDG-PET/CT may be indicated to evaluate for in-transit metastases if the primary lesion is on the extremity [MCC-6, footnote v]
- Routine imaging surveillance recommended for patients at high risk, including stage IIIB or higher and immunosuppression [MCC-6]
- A study of 61 patients with stage III MCC who were clinically asymptomatic and underwent surveillance FDG-PET/CT revealed a recurrence rate of 33%, with sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy of 92%, 93%, and 93%, respectively [323]

**Laboratory Monitoring:**
- Serum MCPyV oncoprotein antibodies: quantitation may be considered as part of initial workup; for surveillance often obtained every 3 months. Patients who test seronegative have a higher risk of recurrence; in patients who test seropositive, a rising titer may be an early indicator of recurrence. Baseline testing should be performed within 3 months of treatment because titers are expected to decrease significantly after clinically evident disease is eliminated [MCC-1, footnote c; 55,56,117-119]
- Circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA): can assess disease burden in both virus-positive and virus-negative MCC and typically becomes positive prior to or at the time of a clinically evident recurrence. For surveillance, this test is often obtained every 3 months [MCC-1, footnote d; 120-122]
- Laboratory studies as clinically indicated (e.g., complete blood count, comprehensive metabolic panel, liver function tests) [MCC-1]

**Supportive Follow-Up:**
- Immunosuppressive treatments should be minimized as clinically feasible in consultation with the relevant managing physician [MCC-1, footnote e]
- Education regarding self-examination of the skin [MCC-6]
- Multidisciplinary consultation recommended at center with specialized expertise [MCC-1 through MCC-6]
- Consider genetic counseling to evaluate for germline mutations in cancer-associated genes in patients <50 years [MCC-1, footnote f]
- Psychosocial support integration into care

timeline.title: First 3 Years (Every 3-6 months)
timeline.body: Clinical follow-up: physical exam including complete skin and lymph node exam; laboratory monitoring: serum MCPyV antibodies and ctDNA every 3 months; imaging as clinically indicated; self-examination education; minimize immunosuppression; multidisciplinary consultation; consider genetic counseling for patients <50 years.

timeline.title: After 3 Years (Every 6-12 months)
timeline.body: Clinical follow-up: physical exam every 6-12 months; continue laboratory monitoring as per clinical judgment; imaging as clinically indicated; ongoing supportive care including psychosocial support.

timeline.title: High-Risk Patients (Stage IIIB or higher, immunosuppression)
timeline.body: Routine surveillance imaging recommended: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (neck CT if primary on head/neck); consider FDG-PET/CT for extremity primary to evaluate in-transit metastases.

timeline.title: Immunocompromised Patients
timeline.body: More frequent follow-up may be indicated. Immunosuppressive treatments should be minimized as clinically feasible in consultation with managing physician.

timeline.title: Ongoing (All Patients)
timeline.body: Educate regarding self-examination of the skin; integrate psychosocial support; multidisciplinary consultation at specialized center; consider genetic counseling for patients <50 years.
